ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Cross Petition Of Beneficiary And Co Trustee Cheri Wilner Kessner For Orders Staying Proceedings And Compelling Arbitration; Or In The Alternative For Order Directing Corss Repondents To Provide Full Disclosure Of Records And Information And Instructing Trustees To Wind Up Administration Of Trust
Off Calendar Jan-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PTR-09-293007 The Miriam Wilner Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Trustee. Bette Epstein for Susan Golden and Ericka Rosenbaltt. Jeffrey Lurner appeared by telephone and Caroline Hinshaw (present) for Cheri Kessner.

The Court signed the ex parte order approving the settlement agreement. Three fee petitions are to be filed in the revocable Trust and are to include both Trusts by February 23, 2011 (one from Ms. Hinshaw, one from Mr. Siracusa and one from Ms. Epstein). Responses are due by March 30, 2011. Replies are due by April 13, 2011. The hearing on said petitions is set for May 4, 2011 at 1:30 p.m. The Court is requiring time records. No page limit is imposed. The Parties are to try sending the petitions to each other by e mail. If unable to do so, they are to be sent by overnight mail. Upon ruling on the Attorney's fees and filing of the final account by Ms. Adler, releases will be sent by Mr. Siracusa. It is not contemplated that the Court will approve the final account, or the Trustees fees or Mr. Siracusa's fees after today. If anyone refuses to sign the releases, Mr. Siracusa will file a petition for approval of the accounting. There will not be any distributions from the Trusts until the accountings done and the releases are signed.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PTR-09-293007 The Miriam Wilner Revocable Trust dated June 12, 1987.

Appearances: Jeffrey Lurner and Caroline Hinshaw for Cheri Kessner. Bette Epstein for and with Susan Golden and Erica Rosenblatt. Lawrence Siracusa for Elizabeth Adler. David Golden also present.

Counsel stated that Mr. Harris is to be given the opportunity to look into the mathematical errors, receive the subpoenaed records, and he has indicated that he could have the accounting by December 2, 2010. Mediation is scheduled for December 8, 2010 before Judge Ross. Mr. Lurner will file an Order to Show Cause if mediation is not successful. The Court will defer on all matters until December 8, 2010. ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Cross Petition Of Beneficiary And Co Trustee Cheri Wilner Kessner For Orders Staying Proceedings And Compelling Arbitration; Or In The Alternative For Order Directing Corss Repondents To Provide Full Disclosure Of Records And Information And Instructing Trustees To Wind Up Administration Of Trust
Off Calendar Oct-06-2010 Continued To Nov-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PTR-09-293007 (Miriam Wilner Revocable Trust).

Appearances: Jeff Lurner and Caroline Hinshaw for Cheri Kessner. Bette Espstein for and with Susan Wilner Golden and for and with Erica Rosenblatt. Lawrence Siracusa for Elizabeth Adler.

Mr. Lurner stated that the plan had not been complied with at all and that he had planned on bringing an Order to Show Cause to Counsel and their clients for failure to comply. Mr. Siracusa addressed Mr. Harris' work on the accounting stating that the date wasn't complied with because Mr. Harris' had missing documents. Mr. Siracusa noted that Mr. Harris is not his client. Ms. Epstein stated that one of the accountings is the sole responsibility of Cheri Kessner who refuses to do an accounting. Ms. Epstein stated that the documents were turned over but they didn't make copies. Ms. Hinshaw stated that they did not have copies. Additional discussions regarding Mr. Harris progress, missing documents, and the accountings were held. Mr. Siracusa stated that if Mr. Harris is missing something that the parties will know, the accountings will be clear. The Court stated since there has been so much effort that it would be foolish to abandon it now and ordered Mr. Harris to produce his draft by October 15, 2010. The Court stated that the parties can file declarations for it to review. The Court stated that the documents are to be collected, taken to a copy service, copies are to be made for Ms. Epstein, Mr. Lurner, and Ms. Hinshaw at the expense of the Trust. The Court referred the parties to a Settlement Conference on November 8, 2010 in Dept. 611 with Judge Jeffrey Ross. The parties are to meet with Judge Ross and then return to Dept. 204 to report to Judge Mary Wiss on November 8, 2010.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Cross Petition Of Beneficiary And Co Trustee Cheri Wilner Kessner For Orders Staying Proceedings And Compelling Arbitration; Or In The Alternative For Order Directing Corss Repondents To Provide Full Disclosure Of Records And Information And Instructing Trustees To Wind Up Administration Of Trust
Off Calendar Jul-22-2010 Continued To Oct-06-10 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 293007 (The Miriam Wilner Family Revocable Trust).

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and Elizabeth Adler (Temporary Trustee). Jeff Lurner and Carolyn Hinshaw for Cheri Kessner. Bette Epstein for Erica Rosenblatt abd Susan Golden.

Counsel had productive discussion in chambers regarding progress on marshalling of information. Mr. Harris reviewed the documents presented to him with regard to the accountings and estimated costs of $20,000.00, $16,000.00 for the accounting on the revocable Trust with a starting date of November of 2003 and $6,000.00 for the accounting on the irrevocable Trust with a starting date of January of 2003. If Ms. Epstein wishes to request additional accountings for other time periods, she may do so at Her and her Client's expense. A declaration under penalty of perjury from each of the three Daughters regarding thorough and diligent search for all records and production of any found documents are to be filed by September 13, 2010. Mr. Lurner is not waiving his right to go forward with his petition for arbitration. Mr. Lurner and Ms. Hinshaw will have the opportunity to order copies of all subpeonaed records or any additional documents relating to the Trust from the copy service. The Parties are to provide copies of any additional accountings. Mr. Siracusa is authorized to advance payment of a retainer to Mr. Harris in the amount of $9,000.00 from the revocable Trust. The matter was continued to October 6, 2010 at 2:30 p.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the accountings and the petitions.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Cross Petition Of Beneficiary And Co Trustee Cheri Wilner Kessner For Orders Staying Proceedings And Compelling Arbitration; Or In The Alternative For Order Directing Corss Repondents To Provide Full Disclosure Of Records And Information And Instructing Trustees To Wind Up Administration Of Trust
Off Calendar Jun-30-2010 Continued To Jul-20-10 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Matter called with case number PTR-09-293007 (The Miriam Wilner Family Revocable Trust)

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and Elizabeth Adler (Temporary Trustee). Jeff Lurner and Carolyn Hinshaw for Cheri Kessner. Bette Epstein for Erica Rosenblatt.

At the initial call of the matter, Mr. Lurner requested an in chambers conference with all parties and the Court. Ms. Epstein and Mr. Siracusa agreed. In chambers conference held off the record. At the second call of the matter, the Court continued all of the petitions to July 20, 2010 at 2:00 p.m. in Dept. 204, stated that an accounting will be prepared, that Mr. Siracusa will contact Martin Harris regarding an estimate for preparing an accounting for both of the Trusts, and that the accountings will be primarily prepared at the the expense of the Trusts subject to reallocation. In addition, the Court stated that Mr. Harris is to receive all documents to date including any additional documents that come in response to any outstanding subpoenas, that Mr. Harris will determine the start date for the accountings based upon the records that are available to him, and that the 7/20/10 hearing will be a status on the estimate of the cost of the scope of Mr. Harris' task. Mr. Lurner addressed the 7/20/10 status date inquiring whether they could still revisit their argument for arbitration if there is no agreement by either of their respective clients. The Court confirmed that there was no waiver by going down this path. Discussions regarding the copying were held. The Court noted that Ms. Epstein volunteered to arrange for the copying of the full sets of documents and that the the expense can be subject to an adjustment. Mr. Lurner inquired whether the copy service could burn a disc of the documents. Mr. Siracusa stated that he did not know whether Mr. Harris wanted a disc but he would inquire.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Cross Petition Of Beneficiary And Co Trustee Cheri Wilner Kessner For Orders Staying Proceedings And Compelling Arbitration; Or In The Alternative For Order Directing Corss Repondents To Provide Full Disclosure Of Records And Information And Instructing Trustees To Wind Up Administration Of Trust
Off Calendar Apr-28-2010 Continued To Jun-30-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 293007 The Miriam Wilner Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Bette Epstein for and with Susan Golden and Erica Rosenblatt (Petitioners). Carolyn Hinshaw and Jeffrey Lurner for Cheri Kessner (Co-Trustee). Lawrence Siracusa for Elizabeth Adler (Interim Trustee for both Trusts).

The Parties discussed the issue regarding the demand for arbitration and the remaining issues. The Parties agreed to the following: 1) to try and resolve the case 2) to take the case to mediation 3) to agree on a Mediator by May 7, 2010 4) that Bette Epstein is to review all documents to ascertain if any other documents need to be turned over and to produce those documents within three business days of receipt 5) that the subpeonas proceed but that all other outstanding discovery be put on hold 6) to particiate in the first session of mediation without taking depositions and that they may proceed with depositions before the second session if needed 7) to authorize Mr. Siracusa to discuss the issue of funding with Mr. Feder and 8) to continue the matter to June 30, 2010 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 to argue the Request for Stay and Demand for Arbitration, and for setting dates on the remaining issues.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For 1)Suspension; 2) Removal; 3) Acceptance Of Conditional Resignation; 4) Appointment Of Neutral Interim And Successor Trustee; And 5) Accou
Off Calendar Feb-09-2010 Continued To Apr-14-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 293007 (The Miriam Wilner Revocable Trust dated June 12, 1987).

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw and Jeffrey Lurner for Cheri Kessner (Co-Trustee/Beneficiary). Bette Epstein for and with Susan Golden and Erica Rosenblatt.

The Court informed Counsel that the documents filed late yesterday had not been reviewed by the Court. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the appointment of a third neutral Trustee, the lack of administration/distribution of the trust assets, lack of cooperation with exchanging of information, venue and the arbitration provision. The Court ordered an interim order as follows: 1)The Court accepts the resignation of Susan Golden and Erica Rosenblatt. 2)Suspends the powers of Cheri Kessner. 3) Appoints Elizabeth Adler as the Temporary Successor Trustee to serve without bond as all Parties agreed to waive bond. 4) All trust documents from all Parties are to be turned over to Ms. Adler and to opposing Counsel by February 19, 2010. The remaining issues on today's petition and the subsequent cross petitions are to be heard on April 14, 2010 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 204. Oppositions are due by March 23, 2010. Replies are due by April 6, 2010.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For 1)Suspension; 2) Removal; 3) Acceptance Of Conditional Resignation; 4) Appointment Of Neutral Interim And Successor Trustee; And 5) Accou
Off Calendar Jan-26-2010 Continued To Feb-09-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 293007 Miriam Wilner Revocable Trust dated June 12, 1987.

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw appearing specially for Cheri Kessner. Bette Epstein for Susan Golden and Erica Rosenblatt.

The Court stated that venue is proper in San Francisco, that Mr. Lurner wanted additional time to respond to the petition, that the Court was inclined to appoint an interim Trustee, to suspend Co-Trustee Cheri Kessner's powers, order bond for Ms. Adler, allow no further distributions and to continue the matter. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the matter being continued/not continued, Client's right to file a petition for removal, the need to determine what should be in both trusts, the stock portfolio not being managed, a neutral Party being appointed, lack of information being exchanged, and the ongoing issues with the Sisters' family dynamics. The matters were continued to Febraury 9, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the trust assets for both trusts.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR #2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
